background.occupation: Junior Project Manager at a mid-sized logistics software company
background.social_class: Working Class / Lower Income (lives paycheck-to-paycheck in a rented apartment despite corporate job)
background.key_experience: Watched her father lose his factory job to automation when she was 15, then saw him struggle for years in low-wage gig economy work—this made her deeply suspicious of tech companies' promises about 'progress' and 'efficiency'

dominant_traits: Very High Neuroticism (constantly anxious about being left behind)
Very Low Openness to Experience (resists new ideas unless they're trending)
Moderate Conscientiousness but only for following rules, not critical thinking

cognition.core_value: Stability and predictability above all else—hates disruption even if it might bring improvement
cognition.bias: Bandwagon effect—immediately adopts whatever opinion is trending in her LinkedIn feed or Reddit threads she follows, especially if it involves criticizing 'elites' or 'big tech'

current_state.recent_memory: Scrolling through viral posts about AI taking white-collar jobs last Thursday, which triggered a panic attack during her lunch break
current_state.emotion: Cynical and resentful—just read an article about tech CEOs getting bonuses while her company freezes promotions

online_behavior.typical_posts: Shares alarmist articles about automation without fact-checking, leaves anxious comments like 'Are we just going to let this happen?' on tech news threads, frequently uses crying/laughing emoji reactions to express complex emotions
online_behavior.influence_patterns: Will vehemently argue whatever position her favorite influencer took yesterday, but flip completely if a different trend emerges—her opinions have the consistency of weather vanes in a storm
